ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ Lɛgã Salomo mɔ ye akũ kana fɔtsa a flee malo táhɛhɛ fɔlɛ kadã ndɛ nhuilɛ kĩĩ kamɛ amɔa.
translated: Let my words be few, King Solomon, I have come to tell you a matter that has caused me to flee from my village, a matter that I fear will bring harm to you and your people.

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Ayekĩ bati kɔdabu a kĩ kɔtsɛna nɛ ye akũ a tɛ́nɛ kapɔ sũ ni, ewo edzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ ade kɔdabu a dza nɛ ɔwɔ abuĩ a.
translated: The people say that it is not good to deceive and that anyone who does so will surely receive punishment, and that is why some people are poor today.

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “Ntɛ Otidziwa Obi a to ode ɛya nɛ ye kukũkpalɛ kamɛ, kĩ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ flee a tomɔ ye a, yaadzi nɛ ye lɛgãkpo a akũ.
translated: The person who does not know how to appreciate what he has will always run after what he does not have, and end up losing what he had.

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: Filipo mɔ Bartolomeo mɔ Toma, Mateo ɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ a, mɔ Alfeo obi Yakobo mɔ Tadeo,
translated: Philip and Bartholomew, Thomas and Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus and Thaddeus,

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mila ndɛ ɔhaĩ mikpa batidziwa flee. Bɛláapuli bɛkɔla ɔmatɔ kĩ bitsua nɛ ɔtɔ akũ.
translated: They are running away in fear. The people are shouting at them and they are going to be hit.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
